name: TransCatalunya and Dragon Khan Loop from Cardedeu
name_original: Parque de Montenegra - Cardedeu, Camps de la Senyora Comtessa, Dragon Khan - circular
description: This moderate 26.4-mile mountain biking loop in Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor features varied terrain and the technical Dragon Khan Trail
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Cardedeu offer diverse landscapes and varied terrain, situated in the Vallès Oriental region of Catalonia. The area provides access to Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve with significant elevation changes and mixed forests, and Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor, featuring wooded trails and hilly terrain. Riders can expect routes traversing forested areas, rural settings, and leading to features like the Vallforners Reservoir, providing panoramic views. Best mountain bike trails in Cardedeu The most popular mountain bike trail is TransCatalunya and Dragon Khan Loop from Cardedeu, a 26.4 miles (42.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route features varied and technical terrain, including sections of the Dragon Khan Trail. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Can Colomer farmhouse – Corredor Sanctuary loop from Cardedeu, a difficult 31.4 miles (50.5 km) path. This route winds through the wooded trails and hilly terrain of Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor, offering cross-country and all-mountain style riding. Local mountain bikers also love the La Torrassa del Moro – Hermitage of Saint Sebastian loop from Cardedeu, a 18.5 miles (29.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes and past historical elements, often completed in about 3 hours 7 minutes. Mountain biking around Cardedeu is defined by the diverse ecosystems of Montseny Natural Park, the wooded trails of Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor, and scenic routes to the Vallforners Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Cardedeu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 90 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cardedeu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cardedeu?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Cardedeu, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the Vallès Oriental region, including parts of Montseny Natural Park and Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Cardedeu?
A: The terrain around Cardedeu is quite varied. You'll find everything from forested areas and rural paths to hilly sections with significant elevation changes, especially within Montseny Natural Park and Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956361178/transcatalunya-and-dragon-khan-loop-from-cardedeu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;TransCatalunya and Dragon Khan Loop from Cardedeu&lt;/a&gt;, feature varied and technical sections.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Cardedeu?
A: Yes, Cardedeu offers several easier mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners. Out of the 33 available routes, 8 are classified as easy, providing a gentler introduction to mountain biking in the region. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails around Cardedeu?
A: Many natural parks and trails in Catalonia, including those around Cardedeu, are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. However, it's always best to check specific park regulations or individual trail rules before heading out, particularly within protected areas like Montseny Natural Park or Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Cardedeu?
A: Mountain biking around Cardedeu offers access to several natural and historical landmarks. You can ride to the serene Vallforners Reservoir, explore the diverse ecosystems of Montseny Natural Park, or discover the wooded trails of Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor. Some routes also pass by historical sites, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1591651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Torrassa del Moro&lt;/a&gt;, which you can see on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/25287963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Torrassa del Moro – Hermitage of Saint Sebastian loop from Cardedeu&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike near Cardedeu?
A: Yes, the region around Cardedeu, particularly within Montseny Natural Park, features several streams and waterfalls. While specific mountain bike routes might not lead directly to every waterfall, you can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3024305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Congost River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7670645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Torrent del Verder&lt;/a&gt; in the broader area, which can be incorporated into or accessed from some rides.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available from Cardedeu?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes originating from Cardedeu are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1471039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Colomer farmhouse – Corredor Sanctuary loop from Cardedeu&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/25269765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallforners Reservoir loop from Cardedeu&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cardedeu?
A: Cardedeu offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for riding. While winter riding is possible, especially on lower-altitude trails, summer can be quite warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during hotter months.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Cardedeu?
A: The mountain bike trails around Cardedeu are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 90 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of challenging and moderate terrain, and the opportunity to explore natural parks like Montseny and Montnegre i el Corredor.

Q: Are there options for advanced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Absolutely. Cardedeu is an excellent base for experienced riders. There are 9 difficult routes available, with significant elevation gains and technical sections. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31233226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of the Roca d’en Toni – Can Boquet Cross loop from Cardedeu&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 48 km route with nearly 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in Cardedeu?
A: Cardedeu is a town with various parking options. Many routes start from or pass through accessible points where street parking or designated parking areas might be available. It's advisable to check the specific starting point of your chosen route on komoot for the most accurate parking information.

Q: Can I reach the mountain bike trails in Cardedeu using public transport?
A: Cardedeu is well-connected by public transport, particularly by train from Barcelona, making it feasible to reach the area without a car. While some trailheads might be a short ride from the train station, it's recommended to plan your route in advance to ensure convenient access to your desired starting point.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Parque de Montenegra - Cardedeu, Camps de la Senyora Comtessa, Dragon Khan - circular route?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is rated as moderate overall, but it requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. Be prepared for varied terrain with constant climbs and descents. Some sections, particularly on the Dragon Khan Trail, can be technically demanding, featuring narrow paths, roots, and rocks, which may require riders to push their bikes (STS S2 difficulty). It's not generally recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 42.5 kilometers long with an elevation gain of about 840 meters. On average, it takes around 3 hours and 45 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, as a circular route, you can typically start and finish at various points. Given its proximity to Cardedeu, look for parking options in or around the town that offer convenient access to the Montnegre area. Many routes in the region begin near local villages.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails within the Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Montnegre area can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, so an early start is advisable, while winters can be cooler and potentially wetter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for the Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for general access to the trails within the Parc del Montnegre i el Corredor for activities like mountain biking. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas and private land.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and points of interest will I encounter?
faqs.answer: This route takes you through the spectacular landscapes of the Montnegre region, known for its ancient forests and charming rural areas. Along the way, you'll pass by the historic Camps de la Senyora Comtessa Farmhouse and the serene Hermitage of Saint Sebastian. The Dragon Khan Trail segment itself is a notable feature, promising an engaging ride through varied terrain.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the TransCatalunya. De la Mediterrània, Xarxa Pedalable del Vallès Oriental, and De la Tordera al Llobregat. Variant Marata - Pla de l'Espinal. It also briefly crosses the GR 92 BTT and El Meridià Verd, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain biking trip?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for the weather. A helmet is mandatory, and protective gear is highly recommended, especially for the more technical sections.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for families or young riders?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate difficulty, significant distance (42.5 km), and technical sections, this route is generally not recommended for families with young children or beginner riders. It's best suited for experienced mountain bikers with good fitness and advanced skills.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenging sections to be aware of?
faqs.answer: Yes, the sections incorporating the Dragon Khan Trail are known to be particularly challenging. These segments can be extremely narrow, unforgiving, and feature technical descents, roots, and rocks. Riders should be prepared for precise handling and potentially needing to dismount and push their bikes through certain parts.
